title: POST-H-019-D — Quality gate plugin safety
commands: python -m pytest -p no:ddtrace tests/test_post_h_019_plugin_quality_gate.py tests/test_post_h_019_plugin_static_validator.py tests/test_post_h_019_plugin_execution_blocked.py tests/test_post_h_019_plugin_permission_model.py tests/test_post_h_019_plugin_sandbox_design.py tests/test_quality_gate.py tests/test_plugin_registry.py tests/test_project_global_state.py tests/test_post_h_018_connector_sandbox_policy.py -q
python -m devpilot_core schema validate --schema-id PostHManifest --instance docs/post_h_019_d_manifest.json --json
risks: Confundir quality gate plugin-sandbox-design con un runtime de plugins.
Relajar deny-by-default para permisos críticos sin ADR futura.
Sobredeclarar madurez productiva antes del runbook final y cierre POST-H-019-E.
limitations: POST-H-019-D integra seguridad al quality gate; no implementa plugin execution.
No hay importlib, subprocess, pip install, marketplace, network, filesystem write ni remote execution para plugins.
Runbook metadata-only final y trigger ADR quedan para POST-H-019-E.
notes: The subgate composes existing validators instead of duplicating plugin safety logic.
Plugin ecosystem eval is consumed as deterministic local fixture signal, not as LLM judge.
Future plugin execution requires ADR, sandbox, RBAC, approvals, tests, observability and rollback.
pass_criteria: Plugin registry, permission model and exposure report are evaluated.
block_criteria: BLOCK if plugin execution is enabled.
BLOCK if critical permissions can be declared without denial.
BLOCK if quality gate stops evaluating plugin registry.
BLOCK if importlib, subprocess, pip install, marketplace, network or external APIs are used for plugins.
